perturbation.original_text: 1.1 TRANSPORTATION QUANTITY - shall mean the maximum daily quantity of gas which Transporter agrees to receive and transport on a firm basis, subject to Article II herein, for the account of Shipper hereunder on each day during each year during the term hereof, which shall be 51,000 dekatherms. Any limitations on the quantities to be received from each Point of Receipt and/or delivered to each Point of Delivery shall be as specified on Exhibit "A" attached hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 TRANSPORTATION QUANTITY - shall mean the typical daily quantity of gas which Transporter aims to receive and transport, subject to operational constraints outlined in Article II herein, for the account of Shipper hereunder on each day during each year during the term hereof, which shall be approximately 51,000 dekatherms. Any limitations on the quantities to be received from each Point of Receipt and/or delivered to each Point of Delivery shall be as specified on Exhibit "A" attached hereto.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'maximum daily quantity' to 'typical daily quantity' and adding 'approximately' and 'aims to', we introduce ambiguity regarding the firm commitment of the Transporter to deliver the stated quantity. This contradicts the original intent of a firm transportation agreement, potentially conflicting with Article II, which discusses firm basis transportation, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE II - TRANSPORTATION Transportation Service - Transporter agrees to accept and receive daily on a firm basis, at the Point(s) of Receipt from Shipper or for Shipper's account such quantity of gas as Shipper makes available up to the Transportation Quantity, and to deliver to or for the account of Shipper to the Point(s) of Delivery an Equivalent Quantity of gas.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'TRANSPORTATION QUANTITY' from a 'maximum daily quantity' to a 'typical daily quantity' and introduces the terms 'approximately' and 'aims to'. This significantly weakens the commitment of the Transporter regarding the quantity of gas to be transported. The contradicted text, Article II, explicitly states that the Transporter agrees to 'accept and receive daily on a firm basis' up to the Transportation Quantity. By making the quantity 'typical' and 'approximate,' the changed text directly contradicts the firm obligation outlined in Article II, creating an in-text contradiction that could lead to disputes about the Transporter's obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 Pursuant to Article VIII, Section 1 of the General Terms and Conditions of Transporter's Tariff ("GT&C"), Transporter shall cause the delivery of natural gas to Shipper at the Shipper's Primary Point of Delivery as nearly as practicable to Transporter's line pressure, provided that such line pressure shall not be less than 500 pounds per square inch gauge at Monroe, meter number 020843, and 600 pounds per square inch gauge at Calvary, meter number 020844. Transporter shall be obligated to provide such minimum pressures only to the extent that capacity is reserved by Shipper and scheduled by Transporter at the Primary Delivery Point(s) described above. Such minimum pressure obligation is subject to the GT&C including, but not limited to, Article X - Excuse of Performances. In the event Transporter is unable to maintain the minimum pressure(s) described herein but Shipper is still able to take receipt of the scheduled quantity at the Primary Delivery Point(s) described above, then Shipper shall be considered unharmed by Transporter's inability to maintain such minimum pressure(s). Subject to the foregoing, any failure on Transporter's part to deliver the scheduled quantity at the Primary Delivery Point(s) 1 described above shall entitle Shipper to the limited remedy specified in Rate Schedule FT-A, Section 7 - Failure of Transporter.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 Pursuant to Article VIII, Section 1 of the General Terms and Conditions of Transporter's Tariff ("GT&C"), Transporter will endeavor to cause the delivery of natural gas to Shipper at the Shipper's Primary Point of Delivery as nearly as practicable to Transporter's line pressure, but makes no guarantees regarding pressure levels at any specific point. Transporter's obligation to deliver gas is contingent upon operational conditions and system-wide demand. Failure to achieve specific pressure levels shall not be considered a breach of contract, provided that Transporter makes reasonable efforts to maintain consistent supply.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'shall cause the delivery' to 'will endeavor to cause the delivery, but makes no guarantees' weakens the obligation of the Transporter regarding pressure levels. By asserting that failure to achieve specific pressure levels is not a breach, even if reasonable efforts are made, it introduces ambiguity, and it contradicts the prior statement that specifies the pressure needed and Shipper remedy, creating an in-text contradiction with Section 7 - Failure of Transporter.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the foregoing, any failure on Transporter's part to deliver the scheduled quantity at the Primary Delivery Point(s) 1 described above shall entitle Shipper to the limited remedy specified in Rate Schedule FT-A, Section 7 - Failure of Transporter.
perturbation.justification: The original text created an obligation on the Transporter to deliver natural gas at a specific pressure, and provided the Shipper with a remedy (Rate Schedule FT-A, Section 7) if the Transporter failed to meet that obligation. The changed text removes that obligation and states that failure to achieve the specified pressure levels is not a breach of contract. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which explicitly states that failure to deliver the scheduled quantity entitles the Shipper to a remedy under Section 7. The changed text effectively nullifies the Shipper's right to that remedy, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The 'Perturbation Explanation' clearly defines this as creating an in-text contradiction with Section 7.

perturbation.original_text: 12.1 This contract shall be effective as of November 1, 2002, and shall remain in force and effect, unless modified as per Exhibit B, until October 31, 2012.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.1 This contract shall be effective as of November 1, 2002, and shall remain in force and effect, unless terminated by either party with a 30-day written notice, or modified as per Exhibit B, until October 31, 2012.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'unless terminated by either party with a 30-day written notice', a new termination clause is introduced that conflicts with the fixed term ending October 31, 2012, and the conditions specified on Exhibit B. This creates an in-text contradiction, making it unclear how the contract can be terminated. It affects any section that refers to the contract's term or termination conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: GAS TRANSPORTATION AGREEMENT (For Use under FT-A Rate Schedule) EXHIBIT "B" TO GAS TRANSPORTATION AGREEMENT DATED November 1, 2002 BETWEEN TENNESSEE GAS PIPELINE COMPANY AND LOUISVILLE GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY BUYOUT/EARLY TERMINATION PROVISIONS* SERVICE PACKAGE: BUYOUT PERIOD(S) November 1, 2007 through October 31, 2012 AMOUNT OF TQ REDUCED 51,000 Dth FOR PERIOD(S) AMOUNT OF $0.00 BUYOUT PAYMENT FOR PERIOD(S) ANY LIMITATIONS ON THE EXERCISE OF THE BUYOUT/TERMINATION OPTION AS BID BY THE SHIPPER: Shipper must notify Transporter on or before October 31, 2006 of its intent to terminate the attached service agreement. Such termination shall become effective as of November 1, 2007. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if at any time Tennessee terminates the July 8, 2002, Negotiated Rate Agreement between Tennessee and Shipper for any reason, Shipper shall have the unilateral right to notify Tennessee of its intent to terminate this Agreement effective sixty days after the referenced Negotiated Rate Agreement terminates. *NOTICE MUST BE GIVEN AS PROVIDED FOR IN THE NET PRESENT VALUE STANDARD OF THE GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the contract remains in effect until October 31, 2012, unless modified per Exhibit B. The changed text introduces a new termination clause allowing either party to terminate with a 30-day notice, which directly contradicts the fixed term ending date and the termination provisions in Exhibit B that specify a limited buyout period and notice requirements. This creates a direct conflict because the contract could now be terminated at any time with 30 days' notice, overriding the specific termination conditions outlined in Exhibit B, which is a clear contradiction.
